sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, angle FLOAT, time_data TIME);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, angle, time_data) VALUES (1, 0.5, TIME '12:30:00'), (2, NULL, TIME '14:00:00'), (3, 1.5708, TIME '06:02:03');
SELECT id, asin(angle) AS arcsine_value, time_data - INTERVAL 2 HOUR AS adjusted_time FROM test_data WHERE angle IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, asin(angle) AS arcsine_value, time_data FROM test_data HAVING adjusted_time > TIME '10:00:00' AND arcsine_value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, asin(angle) AS arcsine_value FROM (SELECT id, CASE WHEN angle IS NULL THEN 0 ELSE angle END AS angle FROM test_data) AS subquery WHERE arcsine_value < asin(1);
CREATE TABLE additional_data (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, time_offset INTERVAL, reference_time TIME);
INSERT INTO additional_data (id, time_offset, reference_time) VALUES (1, INTERVAL '3 HOUR', TIME '08:00:00'), (2, INTERVAL NULL, TIME '10:00:00');
SELECT a.id, a.time_offset, a.reference_time, a.reference_time - a.time_offset AS calculated_time FROM additional_data a WHERE a.time_offset IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, asin(angle) AS arcsine_value, (SELECT MAX(time_data) FROM test_data) - INTERVAL 1 HOUR AS max_adjusted_time FROM test_data;
SELECT a.id, asin(a.angle) + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM additional_data) AS modified_arcsine_value FROM test_data a JOIN additional_data b ON a.id = b.id WHERE a.angle > 0 AND b.time_offset IS NOT NULL;
